next_steps: Send serum for Hantavirus serology (IgM and IgG) and RT-PCR
Monitor fluid balance strictly; avoid aggressive fluid resuscitation
Consider early initiation of extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (ECMO) evaluation for refractory hypoxemia
Perform bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL) for viral, bacterial, and fungal pathogens (if hemodynamically stable)
Check serum haptoglobin, LDH, and schistocytes to rule out microangiopathic hemolytic anemia
Consult infectious disease specialist for endemic tropical/zoonotic diseases

differential_diagnoses: Severe community-acquired pneumonia with sepsis-induced ARDS
Systemic vasculitis with diffuse alveolar hemorrhage
next_steps: Obtain peripheral blood smear for atypical lymphocytes
Send serum for Hantavirus-specific RT-PCR and IgM/IgG serology
Monitor fluid status strictly with bedside ultrasound/limited fluid resuscitation to avoid worsening pulmonary edema
Evaluate for ECMO if refractory hypoxemia persists despite lung-protective mechanical ventilation
Perform urinalysis to evaluate for hematuria/proteinuria and rule out glomerulonephritis
Assess serum haptoglobin, LDH, and schistocytes to investigate microangiopathic hemolytic process
Maintain high-level infection control precautions (contact/airborne) due to potential for Andes virus human-to-human transmission
